title: griffonia icondere
intro: Griffonia icondere is a plant native to West Africa, belonging to the family Fabaceae, and is also known as the African yam or kelat tree. The plant contains a number of medicinal compounds, most notably 5-hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP), a precursor to serotonin that has been used to treat depression and anxiety. Studies have shown that Griffonia icondere may also have other health benefits, including reducing stress and improving sleep quality, due to its ability to regulate neurotransmitter levels. Griffonia icondere is commonly prepared as a supplement or herbal tea, and is also used in traditional medicine in West Africa, where it is valued for its potential to promote relaxation and improve mood.
